methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Vertical Aerospace is a public-stage transportation company that has raised over $400M. The valuation universe for this company is constrained: while five private peers operate in the Transportation sector—including Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft—no public comparables exist in the same vertical. The private peer set, though available, lacks sufficient homogeneity in stage or valuation tier to support a reliable peer-median methodology.

Peer-median valuation was not applicable to this analysis. The Transportation sector contains only one same-stage comparable and zero adjacent-stage or similar-tier peers, falling well short of the three-datapoint minimum required for statistical validity. An all-sector median approach would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of business models, capital intensity, and market maturity across Transportation subsegments.

The analysis is therefore constrained by data insufficiency rather than analytical limitation. To establish a credible valuation range, alternative triangulation methods—such as discounted cash flow modeling, venture-backed exit comparables, or revenue multiples applied to disclosed growth metrics—would be required but are not supported by the data provided. Without additional financial metrics (revenue, EBITDA, subscriber counts, or delivery projections) or a denser peer set with disclosed valuations, a point estimate or narrow range cannot be responsibly derived.
